title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
summary_zh: 需联系医生的警示情况：足部发红/发紫、严重肿胀、冰冷麻木刺痛（非神经阻滞所致），或毛细血管再充盈时间超过2-3秒
text_en: Contact your surgeon if you notice. Your foot looks red or blue. Your foot looks very swollen. Your foot feels cold, numb or tingly, unless you had a nerve block during surgery, making your foot feel numb until it wears off. Or, your toenail takes more than 2-3 seconds to return to a pink color when doing the capillary refill test.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
summary_zh: 术后血液循环的重要性及视觉检查第一步：观察手术侧手部和手指，与另一侧进行对比
text_en: Good blood flow is important for recovery after the surgical procedure on your arm. Step 1. Look at your hand and fingers on the arm recovering from the surgical procedure.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
summary_zh: 正常与异常血流的体征判断：正常应温暖、肤色正常、无明显肿胀；异常表现为发红/发蓝、触感凉冷、严重肿胀，提示血流不良
text_en: Compare your hands. Do you see any differences? Your hand and fingers on the surgical side should feel warm, be normal in color, and have little to no swelling. These signs mean you have good blood flow in your arm. If your hand looks red or blue, feels cool, or has a lot of swelling, your arm may have poor blood flow.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
summary_zh: 第二步感觉检查：麻木和刺痛感可能提示血流不良，也可能是神经阻滞麻醉未消退的正常反应，如不确定应咨询医生
text_en: Step 2. Does your hand feel numb and tingly? A feeling of numbness and tingling can also be a sign of poor blood flow. Or, you may feel numbness from a type of anesthesia called a nerve block until it wears off. If you are not sure if you had a nerve block, ask your surgeon.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
summary_zh: 第三步活动手指：通过活动手指促进血液循环，建议每四小时重复以上三个步骤
text_en: Step 3. Wiggle your fingers. Wiggling your fingers may improve blood flow to your hand. Repeat steps one through three every four hours, or as directed by your surgeon.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
summary_zh: 毛细血管再充盈测试介绍：毛细血管是将富氧血液输送到手指的微小血管，该测试用于评估手术侧手指的血流情况
text_en: Another way to check for good blood flow is by doing a capillary refill test. Capillaries are small blood vessels that carry oxygen-rich blood to your fingers. The capillary refill test allows you to see how well blood is flowing through these blood vessels to your fingers on the surgical side.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
summary_zh: 毛细血管再充盈测试操作步骤：捏住指尖至指甲变白后松开，正常应在2-3秒内恢复粉色，表明血流良好；建议每四小时重复
text_en: Step 1. Gently pinch the tip of your finger directly over the fingernail with your other hand. Step 2. Squeeze until your fingernail turns white. Then let go. Your fingernail should turn pink again within two to three seconds. This sign means that you have good blood flow in your arm and hand. Repeat steps one and two every four hours, or as directed by your surgeon.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
summary_zh: 切口基本概念：介绍切口的定义（手术中在皮肤上的切割），切口边缘的闭合方式（缝线、钉、皮肤胶、皮肤闭合条），以及敷料的作用——保持清洁干燥以预防感染、促进愈合。
text_en: An incision is a cut made in your skin during an operation. The edges of your incision may be held together with stitches, staples, skin glue, or skin closure strips. A bandage called addressing covers your incision. To prevent skin infections, keep your dressing clean and dry. Taking good care of your incision will help it heal more quickly.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
summary_zh: 所需材料准备：列出切口护理所需物品——新绷带、医用胶带卷、干净剪刀、两副一次性手套、两块干净软布或纸巾、一次性塑料袋和废纸篓，并提醒将材料远离切口以降低感染风险。
text_en: Caring for your incision involves cleaning your incision and changing your dressing. You will need New bandages A roll of medical tape Clean scissors Two pairs of disposable gloves Two clean, soft cloths or paper towels A disposable plastic bag And a waste basket. Keep these materials away from your incision to reduce your risk of infection. Prepare materials

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
summary_zh: 准备阶段操作：用肥皂和水洗手30秒并擦干，打开新绷带包装但不触碰绷带本体，剪四段与旧胶带等长的新胶带并挂在桌边备用。
text_en: Wash your hands with soap and water for 30 seconds and then dry them with a clean towel or paper towels. Clean hands are the best protection against spreading infection. Open a new bandage wrapper. Leave the bandage inside the wrapper and be careful not to touch it. Cut four pieces of tape and hang them from the edge of a nearby table or counter. The pieces of tape should be the same length as the old ones around your incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
summary_zh: 移除旧敷料：再次洗手后戴上一次性手套，一手按压皮肤、一手向切口方向撕开旧胶带，取下旧敷料。注意不要撕掉皮肤闭合条（让其自然脱落）。若敷料粘连切口，用温水湿润后再移除。检查旧敷料是否有异味或黄绿色污渍（如有则联系医疗提供者）。将旧敷料放入塑料袋，脱手套后一同丢弃。
text_en: Remove old dressing. Wash your hands again with soap and water and then dry them. Put on disposable gloves. With one hand, press down gently on the skin next to the old tape around your incision. With the other hand, carefully peel away the old tape toward your incision. After loosening all of the tape, remove your old dressing. Do not remove any skin closure strips covering your incision. Let them fall off on their own in about 5-10 days. If your dressing sticks to your incision, don't tear it off. Tearing it off may cause bleeding and delay healing. Moisten the dressing with warm water. Wait for a minute or two until you can easily remove it. Look at your old dressing after you remove it. If the dressing has a foul or sweet smell or it has yellow or greenish stain, call your healthcare provider. Place the old dressing in a disposable plastic bag. Remove the disposable gloves. Throw the gloves and the plastic bag containing your old dressing in the waste basket.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
summary_zh: 清洁切口：仅在医疗提供者指示下清洁切口。洗手后戴新手套，用干净布、温水和（经许可的）温和抗菌肥皂轻柔清除切口周围干燥物质，从靠近切口处向外擦拭，每次使用布的不同部位。不要擦洗或去除痂皮（痂皮保护愈合中的切口），不要擦拭缝线、钉或闭合条上方，不要用酒精、碘或过氧化氢清洁切口。
text_en: Clean the incision. Clean your incision only if your healthcare provider says you should. Your healthcare provider may only want you to change the dressing. Make sure you understand what your healthcare provider wants you to do and be sure to call with questions or concerns. Wash your hands with soap and water and dry them. Put on a new pair of disposable gloves. Always use a clean cloth, warm water, and if your healthcare provider says you can, a mild antibacterial soap to gently remove dried material from the area around your incision. Start with the area's nearest urinsision and wipe outwards or away from the incision. Use a different part of the cloth each time you wipe your skin. When the cloth is dirty, use a new clean cloth. Do not scrub or remove any scabs. Scabs protect your incision while it heals. Allow them to fall off on their own. Do not wipe or rub over stitches, staples, or skin closure strips. Unless your healthcare provider says you should, do not clean your incision with alcohol, iodine, or peroxide.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
summary_zh: 检查切口异常征象：让切口自然风干，同时检查是否存在以下异常——缝线/钉移位、切口边缘裂开、出血、切口边缘超过1英寸的发红、切口周围皮肤温度升高、肿胀、异味、白色/黄色/绿色分泌物。如发现任何变化应联系医疗提供者。未经许可不要在切口上使用药膏、乳液或粉末。
text_en: Let your incision air dry. As it dries, check your incision for the following. Stitches or staples that are out of place. The edges of your incision pulling open, bleeding around your incision, redness that extends more than one inch from your incision edges, increased skin warmth around your incision, swelling around your incision, foul or sweet smelling incision odor, or a white, yellow, or green discharge coming from your incision. Call your healthcare provider if you notice any of these changes. Unless your healthcare provider says you should, do not use, appointment, lotion, or powder on your incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
summary_zh: 敷贴新敷料：脱旧手套丢弃，洗手后戴新手套。拿纱布垫一角、不触碰贴切口的那面，覆盖切口（如仍有渗液可多用几块纱布）。一手固定纱布、另一手用胶带封好四边。若旧胶带区域皮肤发红则换位置贴新胶带；若出现肿胀或水泡，可能是胶带过敏，需联系医疗提供者。脱手套丢弃后再次洗手。
text_en: Remove the disposable gloves and throw them away in the waste basket. Apply new dressing. Wash your hands with soap and water and dry them. Put on a new pair of disposable gloves. Pick up a gauze pad by one corner. Do not touch the side that will be next to your incision. Carefully cover your incision with the gauze pad. You may need more than one gauze pad if fluid is still coming out of your incision. Hold the gauze pad in place with one hand and tape the gauze pad to your skin with the other hand. Make sure that you seal all four edges of the dressing. If your skin is red from the tape on your old dressing, put the new tape on a different part of your skin. If your skin is swollen or has blisters, contact your healthcare provider. You may be allergic to the tape and need to try a different kind. Put your gloves and put them into the waste basket. Wash your hands again with soap and water and dry them.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
summary_zh: 术后警示征象总结：切口边缘轻微发红肿胀或清澈/粉色渗液属正常。但出现白/黄/绿色分泌物、异味、切口形状改变、超过1英寸的发红、皮肤温度升高、出血或肿胀时应联系医疗提供者。出现恶心、呕吐、腹泻或体温达101°F以上也应立即就医。
text_en: It's normal to see mild redness and swelling around your incision edges or a clear or pinkish However, you should contact your healthcare provider if you notice. Any white, yellow or green discharge coming from your incision. A foul or sweet incision odor, the shape of your incision changes. Redness that extends more than one inch from your incision edges, increased warmth of the skin around your incision, and bleeding or swelling around your incision. Also call your healthcare provider if you have nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, or a temperature of 101 degrees Fahrenheit or higher.

title: AND13015 Removing Your Pain Pump
summary_zh: 疼痛泵的基本结构与工作原理：术后疼痛泵通过皮下细管（导管）将麻醉药物持续缓慢地输送到手术切口附近，麻痹周围神经以减轻疼痛。导管连接泵体，管路上附有夹子和过滤器。
text_en: A pain pump is used after surgery to deliver numbing medicine directly to the area that was operated on. The medicine numbs the nerves near your incision, so you have less pain. The medicine continuously flows very slowly out of the pump through a small tube placed under the skin near your surgical incision. The small tube, called a catheter, connects to the pump. And a clamp. And filter are attached along the tube.

title: AND13015 Removing Your Pain Pump
summary_zh: 疼痛泵的使用方式：可将泵放入小袋中随身携带，药物按外科医生设定的速率输送（不可调节），药物在2至4天内输完，泵排空后即可拔管并丢弃。
text_en: You can wear a small pouch to hold the pump so you can go about your daily activities. The pump gives you numbing medicine at a rate ordered by your surgeon. The rate on the pump cannot be changed. You receive the medicine over two to four days. When the pump is empty, you remove the tube and throw the pump and tube away.

title: AND13015 Removing Your Pain Pump
summary_zh: 如何判断疼痛泵是否正常工作：药物流速极慢肉眼不可见，需24-48小时才能观察到球囊体积变化；正常工作的标志是手术部位感觉麻木、疼痛减轻、球囊逐渐缩小、外袋松弛起皱；泵排空时袋子变平，可触及中间硬管，通常需48-72小时。
text_en: How to tell if your pain pump is working? The pump delivers medicine so slowly that you cannot see it moving in the tube. It may take 24 to 48 hours before you notice a change in the size and look of the ball. You can tell when the pump is working if your surgical site feels numb and the medicine reduces your pain. The ball gradually gets smaller and smaller. And the outside bag on the pump gets looser and begins to wrinkle. The pump is empty when the bag looks flat and you can feel a hard tube in the middle. This usually takes 48 to 72 hours.

title: AND13015 Removing Your Pain Pump
summary_zh: 疼痛泵的日常护理与故障处理：每天检查夹子是否通畅、过滤器无遮挡、管路无折弯；若泵或管路漏液、管路与泵分离、或管路从皮肤脱出，均应关闭夹子并联系外科医生，切勿自行重新连接或插回。
text_en: Carrying for your pain pump. Check your pain pump several times a day to make sure. The clamp is open and slides easily. Nothing is covering the filter. And there are no kinks or bends in the tubing. If you notice the pump or tube is leaking, close the clamp and call your surgeon. If the tube separates from the pump, do not reconnect it. Close the clamp and call your surgeon. If the tube comes out of your skin, do not put it back in. Close the clamp and call your surgeon.

title: AND13015 Removing Your Pain Pump
summary_zh: 需要关闭夹子并立即联系医生的警告症状：耳鸣、视力模糊、口中金属味、手指脚趾或口周麻木刺痛、嗜睡或意识混乱、疼痛加重、发热寒战出汗、呼吸困难、管口周围红肿热痛、管口出血或渗液、头晕目眩。
text_en: You should also close the clamp on the tube and call your surgeon if you notice, ringing or buzzing in your ears. Blurred vision. A metal taste in your mouth. Numbness or tingling in your fingers, toes or around your mouth. Feel drowsy or confused. Increased pain. Fever chills or sweats. Trouble breathing. Redness, swelling, warmth or pain around the site where the tube enters your skin. Bleeding or discharge from the tube site. Feel dizzy or lightheaded.

title: AND13015 Removing Your Pain Pump
summary_zh: 疼痛泵的防水与睡眠注意事项：泵、过滤器和管路须防水，不可泡澡或浸水，按医嘱淋浴；睡觉时将泵放在小袋中置于身旁被子上方，保持管路松弛，不可压在被子下、放地上或悬挂。
text_en: Make sure to protect your pump, filter and tube from water. Do not take a tub bath or put your pump underwater and be sure to follow your surgeon's instructions about showering. When sleeping, keep the pump inside of the small pouch and place it next to you on top of the covers. This helps keep the tube loose and in place. Do not put the pump under the covers on the floor or hang it from anything.

title: AND13015 Removing Your Pain Pump
summary_zh: 拆除疼痛泵所需材料：创可贴和垃圾袋。
text_en: Materials for removing the pump. To remove your pain pump you will need an adhesive bandage and a trash bag. Removing the pump.

title: AND13015 Removing Your Pain Pump
summary_zh: 拆除疼痛泵的7个步骤：洗手→松开管口敷料→移除固定胶带→贴近皮肤以90度角轻拉导管（若阻力大则停止联系医生以防断管）→检查管尖黑色标记确认完整取出→用创可贴覆盖管口→将管路、泵和敷料丢入垃圾袋。
text_en: Step 1. Wash your hands with soap and water and then dry them. Step 2. Gently loosen the bandage around the site where the tube enters your skin. Remove it by slowly peeling it back. Step 3. Remove the tape used to hold the tube in place. Step 4. Hold the tube close to the skin. Gently pull the tube out at a 90 degree angle. Do not tug or pull hard. The tube should come out easily. If it feels hard to remove or stretches, stop and call your surgeon. Continued pulling could break the tube. Step 5. After the tube comes out, check the tip for a black mark to make sure you removed the entire tube. Call your surgeon if you do not see the black mark. Step 6. Cover the site where the tube entered your skin with an adhesive bandage. Step 7. Throw away the tube, pump and bandages into a trash bag.

title: AND13015 Removing Your Pain Pump
summary_zh: 次日管口检查：洗手后取下创可贴检查管口部位，若出现红肿、疼痛或发热应联系外科医生。
text_en: Checking the tube entry site the next day. Step 1. Wash your hands again and then dry them. Step 2. Remove the adhesive bandage the next day and look at the site. If you have redness, pain or warmth at the site, call your surgeon.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 心肌梗死的定义与病理机制：心脏某区域血流严重减少，心肌细胞因供血不足而坏死。
text_en: A heart attack, also called a myocardial infarction, or MI, occurs when blood flow to an area of your heart is greatly reduced. When the blood supply is not able to keep up with the demand, the heart muscle cells don't get enough blood to stay alive. The result is death of heart muscle cells in the affected area of the heart.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 心梗后的康复目标与总体策略：保持心脏健康、预防再次心梗，康复时间因心肌损伤程度和个体差异而异；降低风险的三大方向——遵医嘱、改变生活方式、识别危险信号。
text_en: The goals after a heart attack are to keep your heart healthy and avoid another heart attack. Recovery takes time and the amount is different for each patient. It depends upon how much of your heart muscle is damaged and your individual rate of recovery. You may also have had a procedure. This may also affect your recovery time. You can lower your risk of having another heart attack by taking these steps, follow your doctor's instructions, make some lifestyle changes, and know when to seek medical attention.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 遵从医嘱的具体要求：按处方服药、不擅自停药或减量、注意药物副作用、避免自行服用非处方药或补充剂、告知医生其他处方药、按时复诊和定期检查（如血压监测）。
text_en: Following the instructions of your doctor and healthcare team is very important to your recovery. This includes taking your medications exactly as prescribed by your doctor. Don't stop taking your medications or skip doses. Take note of any side effects and tell your doctor. Don't take any over-the-counter medication or supplement without first consulting your doctor. Tell your doctor about any other prescriptions you're taking. Some medications can prevent your heart medicine from working. It's important that your recovery is monitored. Keep all of your follow-up appointments. Have exams done as directed by your doctor. For example, if your doctor has told you to check your blood pressure regularly, do as directed.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 饮食方面的生活方式调整：限制盐和钠的摄入（烹饪不加盐、避免加工食品和快餐、对比食品标签），采用心脏健康饮食（低脂、低胆固醇、高纤维），以维持健康的血压和体重。
text_en: Lowering your risk of another heart attack usually requires some lifestyle changes. Limit your salt and sodium intake by doing the following. Don't add salt when you cook or to food at the table. Avoid processed and fast foods. Compare food labels and choose the items that are lower in salt and sodium. Avoid a heart-healthy diet, including low fat, low cholesterol, and high fiber foods. A heart-healthy diet helps you maintain or achieve a good blood pressure and weight. It also helps prevent other conditions that may further stress your heart.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 其他生活方式调整：限制饮酒、保持健康体重、戒烟并远离二手烟；尼古丁会升高心率和血压，同时减少输送到身体的富氧血液。
text_en: Limit your alcohol intake. Risk your doctor if it's safe to include any alcohol in your diet. Maintain a healthy weight. Don't smoke. Avoid all tobacco products and secondhand smoke. Nicotine increases heart rate and blood pressure. At the same time, it decreases the amount of oxygen-rich blood delivered to your body's

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 心梗后的休息与活动恢复：前四周充分休息、保证睡眠、避免重体力劳动、逐步增加活动量；咨询医生何时可恢复驾驶、性生活和工作；数周后开始心脏康复计划（含监控下的运动训练）。
text_en: Allow yourself time to heal by taking it easy for the first four weeks. Getting a good night's sleep. Rest in the afternoon. Avoid heavy lifting and gradually increasing your physical activity. Ask your doctor when you can resume driving, sexual activity, and return to work. For weeks after your heart attack, you will begin cardiac rehab, which includes a closely monitored exercise regimen.
